fields.description:
## Context / Problem

Even after S3 [rebar:94f9-a9a0-7475-4e75] routes client construction through the registry, the
**differ/apply core still names Jira concretely**: per ADR 0035 §(a), `outbound_differ.py`,
`inbound_differ.py`, `differ.py`, `classify.py`, `reconcile_check.py`, `baseline_shadow.py`,
`apply_inbound_records.py`, `outbound_links.py`, `binding_walk.py`, and `reconcile.py` call
vendor field-mapping / sanitization / ADF helpers directly. Three specific leaks must move
behind the `Backend` port (S2 [rebar:ce4d-c3ca-5412-4798]) for the core to be truly
backend-neutral: (1) ADF size-fitting computed inside the differ; (2) lossy status/parent
mapping rules living in core; (3) duplicated value-maps and link-relation constants. This story
rewires those sites to call the `OutboundMapper`/`InboundMapper`/`FieldSanitizer`/
`IdentityConvention` methods on the selected `Backend`, and moves the Jira-specific logic into
`adapters/jira/`. No files are physically relocated here (that is S5
[rebar:dfb9-d2d1-455d-48d9]); this story changes **who calls what**, not where files live.

Edit-set (rewire to the port; move Jira logic into the adapter):
- `outbound_differ.py`, `inbound_differ.py`, `differ.py` — call the mapper via `Backend`
  instead of importing `outbound_fields`/`adf` directly.
- ADF size-fit currently in the differ → move behind `FieldSanitizer`/`OutboundMapper` on
  `adapters/jira/` (the differ asks the mapper for an already-limit-fit payload).
- Lossy status/parent mapping rules in core → into `adapters/jira/` mapper.
- `classify.py`, `reconcile_check.py`, `baseline_shadow.py`, `outbound_links.py`,
  `binding_walk.py`, `reconcile.py` — depend on the interface, not `adapters/jira/`.
- `apply_inbound_records.py` — replace the hardcoded `"jira"` literal and bare key with
  `RemoteRef`/`IdentityConvention`.
- Single-source the duplicated priority/status value-maps and link-relation constants behind
  the mapper (remove the core copies).

## Approach

Work one leak at a time, each behind held-out characterization tests that pin current
reconcile output. For each site: introduce the `Backend`-method call, delete the direct vendor
import, and (where the logic is Jira-specific) relocate it into the `adapters/jira/` mapper so
the core keeps only backend-neutral orchestration. The `RemoteRef` swap in
`apply_inbound_records.py` replaces string-typed identity with the tuple from S2. Because every
change is behavior-preserving, the existing reconcile characterization suite plus the S2
contract suite are the oracle; the diff should shrink core Jira-imports to zero.

## Scope

- Rewire the 10 differ/apply leak sites in §(a) to the `Backend` port.
- Relocate the three specific leaks (ADF-sizing, lossy status/parent, duplicated maps/link
  constants) into `adapters/jira/`.
- Replace the `"jira"` literal / bare key in `apply_inbound_records.py` with
  `RemoteRef`/`IdentityConvention`.
- **Out of scope:** physically moving `acli*`/`adf`/`outbound_fields`/`comment_limits` and the
  loader/patch-string migration (S5); the registry (S3, prerequisite); the interface (S2,
  prerequisite).

## Testing

- **Tier:** integration + unit, RED-first held-out oracle.
- **Characterization:** the full reconcile suite (outbound + inbound passes over fixture
  stores) produces identical events/outcomes to pre-story `main` — the primary guard that the
  rewire is behavior-preserving.
- **Neutrality assertion:** a test/`grep` gate asserts **no** core module (the 10 sites)
  imports `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.*` or the root vendor modules after the rewire.
- **Unit:** ADF size-fitting now invoked via the mapper still truncates at the Jira limit;
  `apply_inbound_records` stamps a correct `RemoteRef`.
- **Teeth:** revert one rewired site to its direct vendor import and confirm the neutrality
  gate goes RED.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] None of the 10 differ/apply sites import `adapters.jira.*` or the root vendor modules
  (`outbound_fields`, `adf`, `jira_fields`, `comment_limits`, `acli*`) directly — verified by
  the neutrality `grep`/test gate.
- [ ] ADF size-fitting, lossy status/parent mapping, and the duplicated value-maps/link
  constants live only under `adapters/jira/` (single-sourced) — verified by `grep` for those
  constructs returning hits only under `adapters/jira/`.
- [ ] `apply_inbound_records.py` uses `RemoteRef`/`IdentityConvention` with no hardcoded
  `"jira"` literal — verified by `grep`.
- [ ] The full reconcile characterization suite yields identical outcomes to pre-story `main`
  (verified: `make test` green, characterization values unchanged).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all green.
